source.recording: Brian Park and Max Riemer - 2026_05_20 13_29 EDT - Recording.mp4
source.notes: Brian Park and Max Riemer - 2026_05_20 13_29 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Max Riemer (Optimus) ran a screen-share demo of the platform's shipper-profile and prospecting data for Brian Park, who runs a new over-the-road division inside an LA-based international freight forwarder (10,000+ containers/year, 10-12 sellers across 7 branches). Max clearly landed the core value prop — data 'ammo' so reps pitch with real shipper intelligence instead of generic outreach — and covered pricing ($1,500/mo, unlimited seats) and sourcing (15-20B in factored invoices, 62,000 profiles), but the demo used generic example shippers rather than Brian's freight world and repeatedly could not meet Brian's stated top priority: automated/mass cold outreach, which is 3-6 months out. The call closed with a vague, buyer-owned next step — Brian will brief upper management and reply next week while also evaluating competing providers.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max was fluent and prepared through the demo, answered directly and honestly rather than reciting a script, and adapted to Brian's questions; good pitch variation (8.23 st) and steady volume (CV 0.625) support an engaged, attentive delivery.

evidence.quote: What uh CRM do you all use?
I don't think so right now.
we're going to click destination inbound South Carolina. And all of these points are shipping into that facility in South Carolina.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could summarize it in one sentence: Optimus gives reps concrete shipper data (lanes, volumes, contacts) so they prospect with a real value prop instead of a generic pitch, backed by quantified sourcing.

evidence.quote: shipper profiles designed to give sales reps enough ammo for when they're prospecting and when they're making these calls and emails, they actually have a a real value proposition rather than just a generic pitch
See about 15 to 20 billion in transactions a year.

relevance.explanation: The walkthrough used generic example shippers and lanes (New Core steel, San Diego to Dallas) rather than Brian's freight-forwarding/OTR container world; the only tailoring was sizing pricing to his 10-12 users, so the demo never showed value specific to his network or role.

evidence.quote: if I click into new core
I'll use San Diego as an example.

discovery_progression.explanation: Max opened with one good discovery question but did little needs probing afterward and, at the close, accepted a vague buyer-owned 'next week' timeline without proposing or booking a concrete dated follow-up with the decision-maker.

evidence.quote: Can you um can you give me a quick rundown of the company sales reps, y'all's roles, and sort of how you heard about

Max Riemer.coaching_action: Run real discovery before you demo — ask the buyer's #1 priority and current outreach process up front, then tailor every screen you show to their own lanes and commodities (freight forwarding / OTR containers) instead of generic example shippers.

signals.signal: Asked procurement-depth pricing questions, indicating genuine evaluation
signals.quote: 10 to 12 users, one year contract, 1,500 per months. And is there a uh setup fee or the integration fee?

signals.signal: His stated top priority (automated/mass cold outreach) is a fit gap the product doesn't yet meet, tempering interest
signals.quote: the main reason that I was looking for the uh the AI system for the sales tool was to I wouldn't say minimize uh just take care of the all those cold curling cold emails

signals.signal: Actively comparison shopping, so commitment is contingent and low
signals.quote: we actually we do have a couple of uh different meeting as well with the other uh providers

signals.signal: Deferred the decision to management with a vague self-owned next step rather than a firm commitment
signals.quote: So I think I got all the information I need to for me to report to the uh upper management and we'll see how it goes
